reasoning: The question strictly requires a formal declaration of war. In May 2025, India and Pakistan fought their most intense military conflict in 25 years ("Operation Sindoor"), utilizing missiles, drones, and fighter jets. Despite this extreme escalation, neither side issued a formal declaration of war—highlighting the contemporary reluctance to use this legal mechanism. Furthermore, analysis indicates the Indian Constitution lacks a dedicated procedure for formally declaring war. Even with recent bombings in November 2025, no official declarations have materialized. With only 24 days remaining in the year and the base rate of formal declarations of war in modern times being virtually zero, the probability of either nation formally declaring war before Dec 31, 2025 is exceedingly low. The minimum probability of 0.05 is chosen based on system constraints.
